subject: Bows and crossbows
method: full human/LLM read of transcript 059 (not the heuristic)
note: All 34 tiers below were confirmed to match the stored tierlists.json exactly. The 'why' field is new content. Bows are rated mostly for damage (archers are damage dealers); passive-effect bows are prized as off-hand 'stat sticks' on non-archers since weapon passives work from the unequipped slot. Rated by power when acquired.

+1 Hand Crossbow.why: Only because there are so few hand crossbows: it's the best one in Act 1, and two of them is often the highest-damage archer setup (dual offhand attacks + doubled Sharpshooter value); the +1 to hit matters enormously for Sharpshooter builds.

Bow of Awareness.why: A plain shortbow whose passive +1 initiative works even in the unequipped slot — so it goes on a melee character for free initiative; the first initiative gear in the game, and on a d4-rolled initiative that +1 is huge in the brutal early game.

Titanstring Bow.why: +1 longbow that adds your Strength modifier to damage — and Strength is effectively free via Giant Strength elixirs, so it reads as +1 longbow with +5 damage per shot; the best two-handed damage bow in Act 1 and a long-term mainstay.

Darkfire Shortbow.why: The first +2 ranged weapon, plus passive fire AND cold resistance and Haste once/day; archers want more damage, but on a melee character it grants free elemental resistance from an otherwise-empty ranged slot — an incredible value pickup.

Hellfire Hand Crossbow.why: A +2 hand crossbow (upgrade over the +1s) with Scorching Ray and a fire-on-hit-from-stealth rider; the unique effects are mediocre, but hand crossbows get no further upgrades, so this is best-in-slot endgame by default.

Ne'er Misser.why: A +1 hand crossbow dealing force damage (rarely resisted, converts all rider damage to force) plus Magic Missile; basically a force-damage upgrade preferred over a generic +2 hand crossbow unless you're doubling piercing with the Bhaalist Armour.

Hellrider Longbow.why: A +1 longbow whose stats don't matter — the passive +3 initiative (and perception advantage) is the point; best-in-slot in the bow slot for any non-archer, an enormous free bonus (would be S+ if the list had one).

The Dead Shot.why: A +2 longbow with improved crit and your proficiency bonus added twice (≈+4 to hit) on every attack; the author's favored endgame bow — slightly less per-hit damage than Titanstring+Cloud Giant, but far more reliable, so more average damage.

Vicious Shortbow.why: A +2 shortbow whose +7-damage-on-crit is a passive (works from the unequipped slot), making it a best-in-slot off-hand 'stat stick' for any melee crit build that doesn't need the Hellrider's initiative.

Bow of the Banshee.why: A +1 shortbow: hits force a DC12 Wisdom save or Frightened (stops movement — great for an archer), plus a passive +1d4 to attack/damage vs frightened enemies that benefits melee fear builds (e.g. GOO warlock) from the off-hand slot.

Harold.why: A +1 heavy crossbow applying Bane on hit (DC13 Charisma, a weak enemy save, with many tries); spreading a save-lowering debuff sets up your control spells, and it even applies before its own Piercing Shot weapon action.

Least Expected.why: A +2 shortbow granting +1d4 to hit while obscured (stacks with Bless) plus a Blinding Shot action; lower damage than the top setups, but the best fix at this point for an archer who does big per-hit damage yet struggles to land shots.

+2 Hand Crossbow.why: Generic, but the +1 to hit/damage over the Ne'er Misser makes it the pick specifically for a Bhaalist-Armour build doubling all piercing damage; otherwise force damage (Ne'er Misser) is usually preferred.

Blightbringer.why: A +1 shortbow (light on stats) but its crits apply Slow with NO save — and with crit gear an archer crits often, letting you lock down bosses with a powerful debuff they can't resist; great for a crit setup.

Gontr Mael.why: The only legendary bow: +3 longbow with guiding-bolt-on-hit and at-will Celestial Haste (haste with no lethargy downside); only the third-best two-handed damage bow, but the free permanent haste is valuable on archers and non-archers alike.

Light Crossbow.why: A simple d8 ranged weapon (strictly better than shortbows) with the solid Piercing Shot action; the early-game ranged option for simple-weapon casters/fighters, equipped widely for the first few levels and a fine off-hand.

Hand Crossbow.why: The non-magical hand crossbow; since there are so few hand crossbows, your dual-wield users ride this a while, and it's a decent ranged option for a Drow/githyanki who isn't a primary archer.

Gandrel's Aspiration.why: A +1 heavy crossbow granting advantage vs monstrosities (e.g. bulettes) and Turn Undead once/rest; a strong swap-in for the specific dangerous encounters it's built against, while still doing decent d10 damage.

Giantbreaker.why: A +1 heavy crossbow applying Reeling on hit (attack-roll penalty); its reeling resets when enemies miss (worse than the Adamantine Shield's version), but it's good against single-attack high-HP bosses where you can re-apply it each turn.

Hunting Shortbow.why: A +1 shortbow with Hunter's Mark once/rest; often the first magical bow you find and a decent early damage bump if your build lacks Hunter's Mark — replaced once you find a real damage bow.

Hellfire Engine Crossbow.why: A +2 heavy crossbow with a level-4 Lightning Arrow (real damage) and a no-cooldown DC15 dex save-or-take-half-and-pulled-30ft action; cool for pulling enemies through surfaces, but the fixed low DC limits reliability.

Longbow.why: Non-magical d8 with Brace (the best default weapon action — reroll damage, take the higher); worth using very early if you have proficiency, but replaced as soon as a magical bow appears.

Crossbow of Arcane Force.why: A +1 heavy crossbow with Brace, Piercing Shot, and a once/rest +1d4 force damage action; just worse than other Act 1 damage bows, only relevant as a third bow for an unusual all-archer party.

Firestoker.why: A no-enhancement hand crossbow with +1d4 (slashing) vs burning targets; loses the +1 to hit/damage that archers value most (for Sharpshooter), so it's worse than the +1 hand crossbow except in a non-Sharpshooter build.

Fabricated Arbalest.why: A +2 heavy crossbow with a clunky mini-Sunbeam action and a bonus-action 1d4 attack that applies one turn of Radiating Orb; only interesting as extra radiating-orb stacking in a themed build.

Shortbow.why: The weakest bow — d6 damage and the weak Hamstring Shot action; light crossbows are also simple weapons and strictly better, so there's no reason to use a shortbow.

Heavy Crossbow.why: The best non-magical weapon (d10 + Brace + Piercing Shot), but it only shows up late in Act 1, by which point you'll have better magical ranged options — so it never gets used.

+1 Shortbow.why: A generic +1 bow; early merchants sell strictly-superior magical versions at the same place, so there's no reason to use the plain +1.

+1 Longbow.why: Generic +1 with no effect; strictly-superior magical longbows are available from the same early merchants.

+1 Light Crossbow.why: Generic +1 with no effect; better magical alternatives sell at the same merchants, so it's never needed.

+1 Heavy Crossbow.why: Generic +1 with no effect; the merchants selling it also stock strictly-better magical heavy crossbows.

Spellthief.why: A no-enhancement longbow that restores a level-1 slot on a crit once/rest; too situational (needs long days, luck, a bow-firing caster) and low-impact to ever be worth using.

The Joltshooter.why: A no-enhancement longbow generating lightning charges; effectively a +1 longbow with ~+1-and-a-bit damage, outclassed on both damage and hit chance by bows available earlier and at the same time (and it competes with the better Spellsparkler).

+2 Longbow.why: Generic +2; if you want damage you take Titanstring, if you want hit chance you take Least Expected/Dead Shot — no reason to use the plain +2.

The Long Arm of the Gur.why: A +2 heavy crossbow with +1d4 vs undead; even in undead fights it does less damage than the Titanstring or Dead Shot you already have — just worse, so ignore it.
